# ChatGPT — Release Notes

This article tracks notable changes to ChatGPT across web, desktop and mobile clients.

## March 12, 2025

We rolled out a refreshed conversation list with pinned chats, folders, and
keyboard shortcuts for power users. The history sidebar now supports drag-and-drop
between folders and a new full-text search filter that surfaces messages within
specific projects only.

Memory now retains preferences across canvases and code interpreter sessions.
Voice mode latency dropped on iOS thanks to a new audio codec; we measured the
median round-trip below 600ms for English-only conversations.

## February 28, 2025

We've enabled file attachments in the GPT-4o mini tier on free accounts. Plus
and Team subscribers continue to get higher daily caps and priority routing.

A new "advanced data analysis" toggle replaces the old code interpreter switch.
The underlying sandbox now supports arbitrary `pip install` for non-binary
packages and persistent virtual environments across messages within a single
conversation.

## January 15, 2025

ChatGPT Search is now available to all logged-out users without rate limits for
the first 20 queries per day. The search backend now cites its sources inline
with hover-cards that show publication date, author and outlet logo.

Custom instructions now sync between desktop and mobile. The character limit
for each field was raised from 1500 to 3000.
